race.name: UCI Gran Fondo Brasil – Pomerode
race.tagline: South America's Premier UCI Gran Fondo Qualifier in Germany-Heritage Santa Catarina

vitals.start_finish_location: Centro Cultural de Pomerode, Historic Center

climate.overview: Early November in Santa Catarina typically features mild spring weather with moderate temperatures and variable precipitation. Specific meteorological data for race day conditions not available in sources.

terrain.overview: Looped road course featuring rolling hills, winding roads, and scenic views of Santa Catarina. The route combines two significant climbs with a southern loop return, offering varied terrain suitable for endurance cyclists.

adventure.explanation: The event offers a moderately adventurous experience through the Vale Europa Catarinense region with scenic routes featuring German and Italian-influenced landscapes, though it remains primarily a road cycling event rather than an extreme adventure.

altitude.explanation: The course reaches a maximum elevation of 400m, which is relatively modest and doesn't provide significant high-altitude cycling challenges.

climate.explanation: Pomerode's southern Brazilian location in Santa Catarina provides a temperate climate suitable for cycling in November, though conditions are neither exceptionally favorable nor challenging.

community.explanation: The UCI Gran Fondo World Series event attracts both professional and amateur cyclists from around the world, fostering a strong international cycling community with shared passion for the sport.

elevation.explanation: The 120km course features 1,239m of total elevation gain with a demanding 7km climb averaging 4% gradient, providing substantial climbing challenges for riders.

expenses.explanation: As a UCI-sanctioned international event, costs are moderate—neither budget-friendly nor premium-priced—with considerations for travel to Brazil and event registration fees.

experience.explanation: The event delivers a high-quality cycling experience combining competitive racing with tourism through a culturally rich region known for its German heritage and natural attractions.

field_depth.explanation: The UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier format attracts serious amateur and masters cyclists, though it's not exclusively elite-level competition.

length.explanation: The 120km granfondo distance is substantial and demanding, offering a full day of challenging cycling for experienced riders.

logistics.explanation: The event is well-organized with clear infrastructure in Timbó, multiple airport access points (83-640km away), and established UCI World Series organizational standards.

prestige.explanation: As a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier event, it carries moderate prestige with the opportunity to qualify for UCI Granfondo World Championships, though it's not among the most renowned granfondos globally.

race_quality.explanation: The event features a professionally designed course with strategic elevation challenges, loop design, and UCI-sanctioned organization ensuring high competitive standards.

technicality.explanation: The course is primarily road-based with moderate technical demands, featuring sustained climbs rather than technical descents or complex terrain.

value.explanation: The event offers good value as a UCI-sanctioned international cycling experience with quality organization and scenic tourism elements, though international travel costs should be factored in.

biased_opinion.overview: Granfondo Pomerode represents a landmark event for South American cycling, offering the continent's only UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier with genuine championship stakes. The German-heritage setting and well-organized course deliver a premium experience, though the event's relative youth (founded 2023) and limited international profile compared to European counterparts position it as an emerging rather than established prestige event. Best suited for cyclists seeking UCI qualification, South American cycling tourism, or cultural immersion in Brazil's cycling heartland.

final_verdict.one_liner: South America's sole UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier, combining championship-level racing with German-Brazilian cultural heritage in a professionally organized, moderately challenging 120 km loop.
final_verdict.full_verdict: UCI Gran Fondo Brasil – Pomerode delivers authentic UCI-sanctioned gran fondo racing to South America with a clear qualification pathway to the 2026 World Championships. The 120 km course offers balanced climbing (1,239 m elevation) across two distinct sections, supported by scenic Vale Europeu Catarinense terrain and German-influenced architecture. Event organization meets UCI standards with expo, kit pickup, full signage, and athlete support. The Mediofondo option (80 km, 809 m) accommodates older age categories. Participation requires UCI license and personal insurance. As the region's only World Series event, it attracts international competitors but remains less established than European gran fondos. Ideal for cyclists prioritizing championship qualification, South American cycling culture, or European-style cycling in a developing market.

logistics.overview: Event held in Pomerode's historic center with full expo and kit pickup November 7–8. Multiple regional airports serve the Vale Europeu Catarinense. UCI license, valid ID, and personal insurance required for entry.
logistics.accommodation: Pomerode and surrounding Vale Europeu Catarinense region offer lodging options; specific properties not detailed in sources.
logistics.registration_notes: Expo and kit collection November 7–8 at Centro Cultural de Pomerode. Gran Fondo start 07:00, Mediofondo start 07:00–07:10. Time limits: Gran Fondo ~5 hours (24 km/h avg), Mediofondo ~4 hours (20 km/h avg). Valid UCI license, ID, and personal insurance mandatory.

history.overview: UCI Gran Fondo Brasil – Pomerode is the third edition of South America's first and only UCI Gran Fondo World Series event, established to bring UCI-sanctioned gran fondo racing to the continent. Held in the Vale Europeu Catarinense region, a German-heritage area named after Pomerania, the event leverages local cycling culture and European architectural charm.
history.founder: UCI Gran Fondo World Series (organized by team behind successful UCI Gran Fondo Brazil)
notable_moments: 2023: Inaugural edition launched as South America's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier
2024: Second edition reinforced event's status as flagship South American gran fondo
2025: Third edition held November 9 with 3,000+ participants across three editions from 20+ countries
Top 25% finishers in age categories qualify for 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships

course_description.character: Looped road course combining rolling hills, winding roads, and two distinct climbing sections. Starts and finishes in Pomerode's historic center with full UCI road signage and fan-friendly setup. Shared route between Gran Fondo and Mediofondo, with Mediofondo taking a shortcut halfway.

suffering_zones.label: Initial 4 km climb from sea level to ~300 m (repeated after 24 km on return leg)
Mid-course 7 km climb at 4% average gradient to day's highest point (~400 m), most demanding section
Southern loop return to finish with accumulated fatigue

course_description.signature_challenge: The 7 km mid-course climb at 4% average gradient reaching 400 m elevation, positioned as the race's decisive section where performance gaps typically emerge.

guide_variables.field_size_estimate: 3,000+ participants across 2023–2025 editions; exact per-event breakdown unavailable
guide_variables.qualifying_threshold: Top 25% finish in age category qualifies for 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships

non_negotiables: Event is UCI-sanctioned road race (no gravel or mixed-surface options)
Looped course design with fixed start/finish in Pomerode historic center
November timing (early summer in Southern Hemisphere)

citations.title: UCI Gran Fondo Brasil – Pomerode 2025 - Elite Wheels
Granfondo Bogota - UCI Gran Fondo World Series

research_metadata.data_completeness: High for core race details; moderate for climate and registration costs
key_uncertainties: Detailed weather patterns for November race day
Per-event participant counts (only aggregate 3,000+ across three editions available)
research_metadata.verification_status: All core vitals (distance, elevation, date, location, UCI status) verified across multiple sources

videos.title: UCI Gran Fondo Brasil Pomerode.SC - Vídeo Oficial
videos.description:
05 de Novembro de 2023 - Pomerode.SC

O UCI Gran Fondo Brasil chega na cidade mais alemã do Brasil para um evento clássico do ciclismo mundial.

Confira como foi o evento e prepare-se para 03 de Novembro de 2024.

videos.title: Gran Fondo Pomerode - Não acredito que isso aconteceu!
videos.description:
E ai Ciclistas! Tudo bem com vocês?
É isso ai galera, prova concluída e com muitas emoções, foi sensacional!
A prova em si foi bem organizada, o local, Pomerode - SC, tem um diferencial muito grande por ser uma cidade turística e com paisagens incríveis ao redor, e isso tornou a corrida ainda mais especial. Foi dura por ter uma altimetria mais intensa mas foi justamente as áreas de paralelepípedo que quebraram mais, ao mesmo tempo, ofereceu um diferencial para ciclistas com outro nível de técnica, quase um Roubaix.
Mas o mais incrível foi a surpresa final, bom, assiste e me diz o que achou e se você participou ou participaria.

Curte! Se inscreve! Compartilha! E me conta a sua experiência!

#granfondo #ciclismo #estrada #roadbike #competicao

videos.title: UCI GRAN FONDO BRASIL SERIES: Minha primeira prova de ciclismo (112km)
videos.description:
Participando da primeira prova de ciclismo da vida! Meu deus; quanto sofrimento! Mas com certeza voltaria! Vem ver como foi! 

🧢 Roupas de triathlon, ciclismo e corrida! https://kupaa.com.br ; CUPOM: GABI10

videos.description:

Apresentando a Dataro Sports:

Fundada com a rica herança do Clube Dataro de Ciclismo, um dos mais renomados clubes do Brasil desde 2001, a Dataro Sports celebra conquistas como campeonatos brasileiros, pan-americanos e muitas outras vitórias ciclísticas. Esta loja foi criada pensando em você, amante das duas rodas, seja você iniciante ou veterano.

Escolhemos cada produto com cuidado, pensando apenas no que realmente acreditamos. Visite-nos em Curitiba, na Rua Equador 198, Bairro Bacacheri, ou explore nossa loja online: Dataro Sports. www.datarosports.com.br 

Nossa Missão: Aproximá-lo de sua paixão, facilitando sua jornada com a bicicleta e entregando os melhores produtos no menor tempo possível.
Nossa Visão: Ser reconhecida entre as melhores lojas e webstores do Brasil, destacando-se pela excelência em produtos e serviços esportivos.
Nossos Valores: Priorizamos a comunicação transparente, a responsabilidade, a confiança e, acima de tudo, a satisfação de nossos clientes.

🔔 Fique por dentro das novidades seguindo nosso canal.
